title: Star Wars: Where Science Meets Imagination
content:
{{Top|rwm|italics=1}}
'''''Star Wars: Where Science Meets Imagination''''' is a traveling exhibit created by the Museum of Science, Boston, featuring props and costumes used in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' films, but focusing primarily on the science behind [[George Lucas]]' fantasy worlds.

==Props, costumes, models, etc...==
The Museum of Science, Boston owns a model [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]], and an [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] suit used in the creation of the films; these will remain on display in the museum, however used in the traveling exhibit space. Other costumes, props, models, puppets, etc. were also used...

===Costumes===
*[[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] – The Jawa costume was made to fit in with a desert lifestyle that falls between a Tusken lifestyle and that of a Human's.
*Young [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]
*[[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] and Tusken Woman
*[[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] – This costume is gigantic, easily making Han's furry friend seem fearsome, as was planned by [[George Lucas|Lucas]].
*[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]
*[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] (From Ep. III)
*[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] (From Ep. III)
*[[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|Darth Vader's suit]] and helmet
*[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtrooper]]
*[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]]
*[[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]
*[[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]
*[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]

[[File:TarffulAndSalporin-OMSI.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tarfful and Salporin costumes at the Star Wars exhibit]]

*[[Wampa/Legends|Wampa]] – A costume, not a prop. It was used on a specially scaled set and was added to the original scene to make the Wampa look huge. The costume itself wasn't that big.
*[[Cold assault stormtrooper|Snowtrooper]] – The snowtrooper outfit was displayed near the average cold-weather Antarctica exploration garbs used in our world.
*[[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé]] (From Ep. I)
*[[Salporin/Legends|Salporin]]
*[[Tarfful/Legends|Tarfful]]

===Models===
*[[Plug-F Mammoth Split-X engine/Legends|Sebulba's podracer]]
*[[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']]
*[[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]]
*[[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] starship
*[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]
*The [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']]
*[[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]]
*[[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] fighter

[[File:SW-WSMI.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The cover of the book for the exhibit.]]

*The [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] Cruiser: [[Invisible Hand/Legends|''Invisible Hand'']]
*[[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|AAT]]
*[[Droideka/Legends|Droideka]]
*[[Viper probe droid/Legends|Imperial probe droid]]
*[[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droid]]
*[[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|AT-TE]]
*[[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]]
*[[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]]
*[[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]

''Maquettes'' were very small models. Some of these were:

*General Grievous
*[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] riding a [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]]
*[[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] tree
*''Millennium Falcon'' (separate from the larger model)

===Props===
*Luke's [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] [[File:LukesLandspeeder-OMSI.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Luke's landspeeder at the Star Wars exhibit]]
*Many of the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] including [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti's]], [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul's]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]], and many more.
*Several [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], such as an [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] blaster, [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett's]] rifle, etc.
*A [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]]
*A [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] training [[Remote/Legends|remote]]
*[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalkers]]' [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthetic hands]].
*[[Lobot/Legends|Lobot's]] headgear
*A [[FX-7/Legends|FX-7]] medical droid
*A [[2-1B/Legends|2-1B]] medical droid
*An [[Interrogation droid/Legends|interrogator droid]]
*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Macrobinoculars/Legends|macrobinoculars]]
*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] sensor pack
*Chewbacca's wookiee [[Bowcaster/Legends|bowcaster]]
*Wookiee blaster
*Tarfful's rifle

===Puppets===
Several puppets were used in the creation of the Star Wars movies:

*"Naked" C-3PO
*[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]

Other puppets were used, but these two were put in the exhibit.

==Contents==
The exhibit features a reproduction of the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]''{{'s}} cockpit, placed within a miniature planetarium, and programmed to simulate the appearance, of jumping to [[Wikipedia:Interstellar travel|light speed]]. The exhibit explains the basics of the argument, according to [[Wikipedia:Albert Einstein|Einstein's]] theories, that [[Wikipedia:faster-than-light|faster-than-light]] travel is impossible. Hosted by [[Anthony Daniels]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], the show also travels through the [[Wikipedia:Oort Cloud|Oort Cloud]] and outside the galaxy to view many other galaxies discovered outside our own and some of the older parts of the universe.

Another ''[[Star Wars]]'' element the exhibit discusses is the [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]], and various forms of real hovercraft technology. [[Wikipedia:Magnetic levitation train|Maglev trains]], air-cushion hovercrafts, and the unusual magnetic properties of super-cooled [[Wikipedia:yttrium|yttrium]] are all discussed, and how they tie in to the technology in the movies.

[[File:AnakinsProstheticHand-OMSI.jpg|thumb|left|280px|Anakin's prosthetic hand as seen at the Star Wars exhibit. This was displayed near a stand with medical connections to our world.]]

The difficulties of creating functional humanoid robots is also tackled, through a number of hands-on-activities. This could go along with a discussion of real, present-day robots, and why, more often than not, they are not built to be humanoid. Several displays of real robots in our world are included.

Medical technology and its connections to medical technology in the movies are shown. A stand displays real false limbs next to the [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthetic hands]] for Anakin and Luke in the movies.

==Tour schedule==
*[[Wikipedia:Museum of Science, Boston|Museum of Science]], Boston, Massachusetts — scheduled to open October 27, [[2005]].
*[[Wikipedia:Museum of Science of Industry (Chicago)|Chicago Museum of Science and Industry]], Chicago, Illinois — October 2007 - January 6, 2008
*[[Wikipedia:Scienceworks (Melbourne)|Scienceworks]], Melbourne, Victoria — 4 June, 2008 - 8 November, 2008
*[[Wikipedia:Center of Science and Industry|COSI]], Columbus, Ohio
*[[Wikipedia:Oregon Museum of Science and Industry|Oregon Museum of Science and Industry]], Portland, Oregon
*[[Wikipedia:California Science Center|California Science Center]], Los Angeles, California
*[[Wikipedia:Science Museum of Minnesota|Science Museum of Minnesota]], Saint Paul, Minnesota
*[[Wikipedia:Franklin Institute|Franklin Institute]], Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
*[[Wikipedia:Fort Worth Museum of Science History|Fort Worth Museum of Science History]], Fort Worth, Texas
*[[Wikipedia:Marshall Space Flight Center|Marshall Space Flight Center]], Huntsville, AL
*Lafayette Science Museum, Lafayette, LA: October 2010 - January 17, 2011
*[[Wikipedia:Pacific Science Center|Pacific Science Center]], Seattle, WA: March 19 - May 8, 2011
*[[Wikipedia:The Health Museum|The Health Museum]], Houston, TX: June 18 - September 18, 2011
*Discovery Science Center, Santa Ana, CA: November 18, 2011 - April 15, 2012
*[[Wikipedia:Exploration Place|Exploration Place]], Wichita, KS: May 26 - September 3, 2012
*[[Wikipedia:Orlando Science Center|Orlando Science Center]], Orlando, FL: October 13, 2012 - March 17, 2013
*[[Wikipedia:Indiana State Museum|Indiana State Museum]], Indianapolis, IN: May 25 - September 2, 2013
*[[Wikipedia:The Tech Museum of Innovation|The Tech Museum of Innovation]], San Jose, CA: October 19, 2013 - February 23, 2014
